---
title: "Xorg won't validate any modes"
date: 2008-09-27
forum: General Help
---

### Post by ms4242 on 2008-09-27
Hi all -

I'm working on setting up ubuntu 8.04 on a dual-boot with XP.  Everything setup alright, but I can't get my resolution up past 800x600.  My monitor is a HSD AH191 and my card is a GeForce4 MX 440 with AGP8X.

Here's the Xorg.conf:

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc101"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver "nvidia"
	Option "NoLogo" "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
	Option          "DPMS"
	Modeline	"1440x900" 106.500 1440 1536 1688 1904 900 903 909 934 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	SubSection 	"Display"
		Modes	"1440X900"
	EndSubSection
	Option "ModeValidation" "NoDFPNativeResolutionCheck" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
EndSection
```


Here's what my Xorg.0.log looks like:

It appears the hardware is being detected, but something is off in the configuration.  I was able to stip out my monitors modeline from by using my windows partion and Xorg still seems to be ignoring the mode.  I believe the drivers are setup right (nvidia 96.43.05 set up through envy) because I'm able to enable desktop effects, but I could be wrong there.  I've tried exporting my EDID from NVIDIA servers settings and by using read-edid it appears my checksum my be wrong (although I have also read that read-edid may be buggy).  I don't know how to 100% verify my edid and if it's off fix it.  So, yup, that's where I'm at.  I'm thinking it's my edid checksum, but I'm not sure.  Any advice on the situation would be much appreciated.

-ms

---

### Post by mawith on 2008-09-27
try this :
sudo apt-get install nvidia-settings

sudo nvidia-settings

---
